header.run_as_admin_title: System variables are read-only. Restart as administrator to edit them.

path_banner.prefix: Envarly is not in your {{scope}} PATH — add it to run

detail.empty: Select a variable to view and edit it

staged.critical_one: ⚠ Critical system variable will be modified
staged.critical_other: ⚠ Critical system variables will be modified
staged.critical_detail_one: {{vars}} — changing this can break Windows or running applications. A snapshot is strongly recommended.
staged.critical_detail_other: {{vars}} — changing these can break Windows or running applications. A snapshot is strongly recommended.
staged.broadcast: These changes will be written to the Windows registry and broadcast to running applications.

diff.title: External changes detected
diff.description: The registry was modified outside Envarly. Check the changes you want to accept, uncheck to revert them.

import.no_content: Paste or upload a file first.
import.no_vars: No environment variables found in file.
import.merge_desc: Add and update selected variables. Variables not in this file are left unchanged.
import.replace_desc: Make the selected scope(s) exactly match this file. Variables currently in the scope but not selected here will be deleted.
import.replace_warning: Replace will delete all {{scopes}} variables not present in your selection. This cannot be undone — consider creating a snapshot first.
import.result_one: {{count}} variable staged{{extra}}. Use "Apply staged" to commit.
import.result_other: {{count}} variables staged{{extra}}. Use "Apply staged" to commit.

export.secret_warning: {{services}} credentials will be included in the exported file. Only export to destinations you trust.

var_table.secret_one: {{count}} selected variable may contain sensitive data (tokens, keys, passwords). Verify you trust the destination before sharing this file.
var_table.secret_other: {{count}} selected variables may contain sensitive data (tokens, keys, passwords). Verify you trust the destination before sharing this file.

snapshot.title: Snapshots
snapshot.description: Save the current state of all environment variables. Preview and restore any snapshot.
snapshot.no_snapshots: No snapshots yet. Create one before making changes!
snapshot.comparing_hint: Select a snapshot to compare with {{label}}
snapshot.need_two: Need at least 2 snapshots to compare
snapshot.matches_current: "{{label}}" matches current state — nothing to stage.
snapshot.staged_one: "{{label}}" staged ({{count}} change). Use "Apply staged" to commit.
snapshot.staged_other: "{{label}}" staged ({{count}} changes). Use "Apply staged" to commit.

snapshot_preview.no_diff: Current state matches this snapshot — no changes would be made.

snapshot_compare.heading: Comparing snapshots
snapshot_compare.no_diff: These two snapshots are identical — no differences found.

env_desc.PATH: Directories searched for executable files. Programs like Node, Python, and Git are found here.
env_desc.PATHEXT: File extensions treated as executables by the shell (e.g. .COM;.EXE;.BAT).
env_desc.SYSTEMROOT: Root directory of the Windows installation (typically C:\Windows). Same as WINDIR.
env_desc.WINDIR: Root directory of the Windows installation (typically C:\Windows). Same as SYSTEMROOT.
env_desc.COMSPEC: Path to the command interpreter (cmd.exe). Used by shell() calls and scripts.
env_desc.TEMP: Directory for temporary files. Many programs write scratch data here.
env_desc.TMP: Fallback directory for temporary files; same purpose as TEMP.
env_desc.USERPROFILE: Current user's home directory (e.g. C:\Users\Alice). Maps to ~ in shells.
env_desc.HOMEPATH: Home directory path relative to the drive (e.g. \Users\Alice). Prefer USERPROFILE.
env_desc.HOMEDRIVE: Drive letter of the user's home directory (e.g. C:). Used together with HOMEPATH.
env_desc.APPDATA: Roaming app data folder for the current user. Suitable for settings that sync across machines.
env_desc.LOCALAPPDATA: Local (non-roaming) app data folder. Used for caches and large data that should not sync.
env_desc.PROGRAMFILES: Default install directory for 64-bit programs (e.g. C:\Program Files).
env_desc.PROGRAMFILES(X86): Default install directory for 32-bit programs on 64-bit Windows.
env_desc.PROGRAMDATA: Per-machine app data shared across all users (replaces the older All Users profile).
env_desc.COMPUTERNAME: NetBIOS name of the current machine. Used by network tools and scripts.
env_desc.USERNAME: Login name of the current user. Read-only; set by Windows at logon.
env_desc.USERDOMAIN: Domain or workgroup the current user belongs to.
env_desc.OS: Operating system identifier (always Windows_NT on modern Windows). Used by legacy scripts.
env_desc.PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE: CPU architecture of the current process (AMD64, x86, ARM64). Differs for 32-bit processes on 64-bit OS.
env_desc.NUMBER_OF_PROCESSORS: Number of logical CPU cores. Useful for setting parallelism in build tools.
env_desc.SYSTEMDRIVE: Drive letter where Windows is installed (e.g. C:).
env_desc.PUBLIC: Shared public profile directory accessible to all users (e.g. C:\Users\Public).
env_desc.ALLUSERSPROFILE: Legacy alias for C:\ProgramData. Points to the per-machine shared data directory.
env_desc.ONEDRIVE: Path to the user's OneDrive sync folder. Set by the OneDrive client.
env_desc.PSMODULEPATH: Semicolon-separated list of directories where PowerShell looks for modules.
env_desc.DRIVERDATA: Directory for driver data files shared between kernel and user mode.
env_desc.HTTP_PROXY: HTTP proxy URL used by curl, wget, Python requests, and many CLI tools.
env_desc.HTTPS_PROXY: HTTPS proxy URL. Takes precedence over HTTP_PROXY for HTTPS requests.
env_desc.NO_PROXY: Comma-separated list of hostnames/domains that bypass the proxy.
env_desc.ALL_PROXY: Catch-all proxy for protocols not covered by HTTP_PROXY or HTTPS_PROXY.
env_desc.SSL_CERT_FILE: Path to a CA certificate bundle. Overrides the default trust store for SSL/TLS verification.
env_desc.CURL_CA_BUNDLE: Path to a CA certificate bundle used by libcurl (curl, Python requests, etc.).
env_desc.REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE: Path to a CA bundle used specifically by the Python requests library.
env_desc.JAVA_HOME: JDK/JRE installation root. Referenced by Maven, Gradle, IDEs, and any tool that invokes Java.
env_desc.CLASSPATH: Semicolon-separated list of JAR/class directories on the Java class search path.
env_desc.JVM_OPTS: JVM flags applied to all Java processes (e.g. heap size -Xmx). Tool-specific overrides exist.
env_desc.MAVEN_HOME: Maven installation directory. Used by the mvn wrapper to locate the embedded Maven.
env_desc.MAVEN_OPTS: JVM options passed to the Maven process (e.g. -Xmx512m).
env_desc.GRADLE_HOME: Gradle installation directory. Referenced by the gradle wrapper.
env_desc.GRADLE_OPTS: JVM options for the Gradle daemon (e.g. -Xmx2g -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=512m).
env_desc.PYTHONPATH: Semicolon-separated directories added to Python's module search path before the standard library.
env_desc.PYTHONHOME: Alternate Python installation root. Overrides the default standard library location.
env_desc.VIRTUAL_ENV: Path to the currently active virtualenv. Set automatically when you run activate.
env_desc.CONDA_DEFAULT_ENV: Name of the currently active conda environment.
env_desc.CONDA_PREFIX: Path to the currently active conda environment directory.
env_desc.PYENV_ROOT: Directory where pyenv stores Python installations and shims.
env_desc.PIP_INDEX_URL: Custom PyPI index URL. Useful for private package registries or mirrors.
env_desc.PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE: Prevents Python from writing .pyc bytecode files when set to 1. Useful in containers.
env_desc.PYTHONUNBUFFERED: Disables output buffering for stdout/stderr when set to 1. Ensures logs appear immediately.
env_desc.NODE_PATH: Semicolon-separated directories searched for Node.js modules before node_modules.
env_desc.NODE_ENV: Conventional runtime mode flag (production/development/test). Libraries change behaviour based on this.
env_desc.NODE_OPTIONS: Command-line options applied to every Node.js process (e.g. --max-old-space-size=4096).
env_desc.NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX: Directory where npm installs global packages and binaries.
env_desc.NVM_SYMLINK: Symlink pointing to the currently active Node.js version under nvm-windows.
env_desc.PNPM_HOME: Directory where pnpm stores globally installed package binaries.
env_desc.GOPATH: Go workspace root. Contains src/, pkg/, and bin/; also used for go install output.
env_desc.GOROOT: Go installation directory. Set automatically by most installers; rarely needs manual configuration.
env_desc.GOBIN: Directory where go install places compiled binaries. Defaults to $GOPATH/bin.
env_desc.GOPROXY: Proxy URL for downloading Go modules. Default is proxy.golang.org; set to a private proxy as needed.
env_desc.GOMODCACHE: Directory where Go stores downloaded module source code. Defaults to $GOPATH/pkg/mod.
env_desc.CGO_ENABLED: Enables (1) or disables (0) cgo. Disable for fully static cross-compiled binaries.
env_desc.GOPRIVATE: Comma-separated module path prefixes treated as private; skips proxy and checksum database.
env_desc.GONOSUMDB: Module prefixes that bypass the Go checksum database. Subset of GOPRIVATE.
env_desc.GOFLAGS: Default flags applied to every go command. Useful for setting -mod=vendor globally.
env_desc.CARGO_HOME: Cargo home directory containing the registry cache, crate sources, and installed binaries.
env_desc.RUSTUP_HOME: Directory where rustup stores toolchains and components.
env_desc.RUSTFLAGS: Compiler flags passed to every rustc invocation in the workspace.
env_desc.RUST_LOG: Log filter for the env_logger crate (e.g. debug, myapp=trace). Used by many Rust applications.
env_desc.RUST_BACKTRACE: Enables panic backtraces. Set to 1 for basic or full for complete traces.
env_desc.CARGO_TARGET_DIR: Override for the build output directory. Useful to share a target dir across workspaces.
env_desc.DOTNET_ROOT: .NET runtime installation root. Used by the dotnet CLI to locate runtimes and SDKs.
env_desc.NUGET_PACKAGES: Directory where NuGet caches downloaded packages. Defaults to %USERPROFILE%\.nuget\packages.
env_desc.DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT: Set to 1 to disable telemetry collection by the .NET CLI.
env_desc.DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT: Runtime environment name (Development/Staging/Production). Overrides appsettings selection.
env_desc.ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT: ASP.NET Core environment name. Controls which appsettings.{env}.json and middleware are loaded.
env_desc.ASPNETCORE_URLS: Semicolon-separated URLs the ASP.NET Core server listens on (e.g. http://localhost:5000).
env_desc.CC: Default C compiler command. Respected by make, cmake, and autotools.
env_desc.CXX: Default C++ compiler command. Respected by make, cmake, and autotools.
env_desc.CFLAGS: Compiler flags for C compilation (e.g. -O2 -Wall). Passed to CC by most build systems.
env_desc.CXXFLAGS: Compiler flags for C++ compilation. Passed to CXX by most build systems.
env_desc.LDFLAGS: Linker flags (e.g. -L/usr/local/lib). Passed during the link step.
env_desc.INCLUDE: Semicolon-separated directories searched for header files by MSVC.
env_desc.LIB: Semicolon-separated directories searched for import libraries by the MSVC linker.
env_desc.ANDROID_HOME: Android SDK installation root. Used by Gradle, Flutter, and React Native build tools.
env_desc.ANDROID_SDK_ROOT: Alias for ANDROID_HOME. Preferred name in newer Android tooling.
env_desc.ANDROID_NDK_HOME: Android NDK installation directory. Required for building native (C/C++) Android code.
env_desc.GIT_EDITOR: Editor invoked by git for commit messages and interactive rebase. Overrides core.editor.
env_desc.GIT_SSH_COMMAND: SSH command used by Git for remote operations (e.g. to specify a key file).
env_desc.GIT_HTTP_PROXY_AUTHMETHOD: Authentication method for HTTPS Git operations through a proxy.
env_desc.GIT_AUTHOR_NAME: Author name for new commits. Overrides user.name in .gitconfig.
env_desc.GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL: Author email for new commits. Overrides user.email in .gitconfig.
env_desc.GIT_COMMITTER_NAME: Committer name recorded in new commits. Overrides user.name in .gitconfig.
env_desc.GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL: Committer email recorded in new commits. Overrides user.email in .gitconfig.
env_desc.DOCKER_HOST: Docker daemon socket or TCP address. Points the CLI to a remote or alternative daemon.
env_desc.DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY: Enables TLS verification when communicating with a remote Docker daemon.
env_desc.DOCKER_CERT_PATH: Directory containing TLS certificates for secure Docker daemon connections.
env_desc.COMPOSE_FILE: Path(s) to the Docker Compose file(s) used when running docker compose commands.
env_desc.DOCKER_BUILDKIT: Enables BuildKit (next-generation Docker build backend) when set to 1.
env_desc.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: AWS access key for authenticating SDK and CLI requests. Pair with AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.
env_desc.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: Secret key paired with AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID for AWS authentication.
env_desc.AWS_SESSION_TOKEN: Temporary session token for assumed roles or federated access.
env_desc.AWS_REGION: Default AWS region for API calls (e.g. us-east-1). Overrides the profile's region.
env_desc.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: Fallback default region. AWS_REGION takes precedence when both are set.
env_desc.AWS_PROFILE: Named profile from ~/.aws/credentials to use. Overrides the [default] profile.
env_desc.GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS: Path to a service account JSON key file. Used by Google Cloud client libraries for authentication.
env_desc.GCLOUD_PROJECT: Default GCP project ID used by gcloud commands and some client libraries.
env_desc.CLOUDSDK_CORE_PROJECT: Explicit project override for the Cloud SDK. Same purpose as GCLOUD_PROJECT.
env_desc.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID: Default Azure subscription ID used by the Azure CLI and SDKs.
env_desc.AZURE_TENANT_ID: Azure Active Directory tenant ID for authentication.
env_desc.AZURE_CLIENT_ID: Service principal client (application) ID for Azure SDK authentication.
env_desc.AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET: Secret for the service principal identified by AZURE_CLIENT_ID.
env_desc.EDITOR: Preferred terminal text editor. Used by git, cron, and many shell utilities.
env_desc.VISUAL: Preferred visual (GUI-capable) editor. Falls back to EDITOR for terminal contexts.
env_desc.DATABASE_URL: Database connection string. Conventional variable used by ORMs and frameworks like Rails and Django.
env_desc.DEBUG: Debug logging namespace filter for the debug npm package and compatible tools (e.g. express:*).
env_desc.LOG_LEVEL: Logging verbosity threshold (e.g. debug, info, warn, error) for the running application.
env_desc.PORT: TCP port the server process should listen on. Used by many web frameworks by convention.
env_desc.HOST: Hostname or IP address the server should bind to (e.g. 0.0.0.0 or localhost).
env_desc.TZ: IANA timezone name (e.g. America/New_York). Overrides the system timezone for the process.
env_desc.LANG: Default locale for the process (e.g. en_US.UTF-8). Affects character encoding and sorting.
